factors.summary: The company is implementing a multi-year restructuring program aimed at reducing costs and improving efficiency, with expected savings of $200 million by 2023. evidence.text: Restructuring charges incurred under this program were $187 in the second quarter of 2020. These restructuring activities are expected to incur additional expenses of $33 in the second half of 2020, $55 in 2021, and $5 in 2022, primarily comprised of plant closing costs, resulting in total estimated charges of $280 for the entire program.

The increase in Total corporate expense for the second quarter and first six months of 2020 was primarily due to the multi-year restructuring program... The increase in Other expense - net is primarily due to higher acquisition and divestiture charges.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Cost actions are being taken but have caused short-term expense increases.

factors.summary: Eaton has been highly active in mergers and acquisitions, including notable acquisitions and divestitures. The company has completed significant divestitures, such as the sale of its Lighting Business for $1.4 billion and expects to complete the sale of its Hydraulics Business for $3.3 billion. Additionally, Eaton has made strategic acquisitions, including Power Distribution, Inc. and Souriau-Sunbank.

evidence.text: Eaton completed the $1.4 billion sale of our Lighting Business on March 2, 2020 and expects to complete the sale of our Hydraulics Business for $3.3 billion in cash by the end of the first quarter of 2021.

On February 25, 2020, Eaton acquired Power Distribution, Inc. On March 2, 2020, Eaton sold its Lighting business to Signify N.V. On January 21, 2020, Eaton entered into an agreement to sell its Hydraulics business to Danfoss A/S.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Eaton is active in strategic M&A, including significant divestitures and acquisitions.
